paper.title: Probing the non-thermal physics of stellar bow shocks using radio observations

extraction.summary: This paper studies the non-thermal radio emission from bow shocks produced by massive runaway stars. The targets include five systems: BD+43°3654, BD+60°2522, G1, G3, and Vela X-1, all 'runaway' stars ejected from their birth clusters/associations. All stars have peculiar velocities in the range of 20-65 km/s — far below the Galactic escape velocity (~500 km/s). The paper defines 'runaway' as stars ejected from their birth clusters, not as Galactic-unbound objects. The paper never discusses hypervelocity stars (HVS), Galactic escape velocity, or Galactic-unbound status. No HVS candidates.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Five massive runaway star systems with bow shocks: BD+43°3654 (O4If, V_star~50 km/s), BD+60°2522 (O6.5V, V_star~42 km/s), G1 (O7V, V_star~20 km/s), G3 (O5V, V_star~22 km/s), Vela X-1 (B0.5Ia, V_star~65 km/s). All were ejected from their birth clusters or associations.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: All stars are 'runaway stars' (cluster runaways), ejected from their birth clusters/associations, with peculiar velocities of only 20-65 km/s, far below the Galactic escape velocity. The paper studies the physics of particle acceleration and non-thermal radiation in the bow shocks of these stars, and is completely unrelated to HVS candidate searches, Galactic escape, or gravitational-unbound discussions.

source_refs.context: Abstract: 'Massive runaway stars produce bow shocks in the interstellar medium.' — defines the targets as runaway stars, not HVS.
BD+43°3654 (O4If): 'The star was ejected from the Cygnus OB2 association' — peculiar velocity ~50 km/s.
G1: 'ejected from the star-forming region NGC 6357' — V_star ~20 km/s.
G3: 'also ejected from NGC 6357' — V_star ~22 km/s.
Model description: 'runaway stars are ejected from their birth clusters at high velocities and are usually isolated' — 'high velocities' here refers to cluster ejection, not Galactic escape.
